prompts: Gene splicing and insertion in genetic engineering involve cutting DNA segments and inserting them into the host genome to modify or enhance desired traits. The process begins with identifying the desired gene to be spliced. Restriction enzymes cut the DNA at specific sequences, while ligase enzymes facilitate the insertion of these genes into plasmids or vectors. These vectors transfer the spliced genes into a host organism, where they integrate into the genome. Selectable marker genes identify successfully modified organisms. The modified organism is then screened and tested for the expression of the desired trait, completing the gene splicing and insertion process.
